- As a general rule, with 20deg average daily temperature;
- Vertical formworks can be stripped on the second day. Formwork removal shall be carried out with extra care to avoid any damage to the concrete member.
- The formworks between the supporting elements at the beam soffit can be removed on the 5
- th
- day. However, considering the method that has been used to support them, it is not possible to remove the formwork without removing the support. (See attached drawings)
- Supporting member can be removed on the 13th day.
- However, we may respond as follow;
- The formworks and temporary supports to be designed by the constructor. The removal time and removal sequence to be checked by the formwork designer and to be documented in the project QA pack.

- Blinding and sulfate resistance
- BRE Special Digest 1, Section D6.5 Provide a sacrificial layer (APM4) notes that if the blinding is to be adopted as an Additional Protective Measure, the blinding concrete should be at least 50mm thick and of the same quality as the foundation construction. Therefore, if the blinding is being used as an APM it needs to be of the same quality as the foundation, but if this is not the case and the blinding is only used as a working platform, it does not have to be sulfate resistant.
- Similarly, BS 8500-1:2023 cl A.4.7 notes that ‘other than blinding or similar applications, standardized prescribed concretes (ST) should not be used where the presence of sulfates or other aggressive chemicals in the groundwater, the ground or any adjacent material give a ACEC classification higher than AC-1.´ Again this im

- Bridge-manual-pdf-complete-v3.4
- The Whanawhana Bridge is a single-lane structure located on a non–state highway rural road with low traffic volumes and a low-speed environment. In consideration of these site conditions, Clause B3.1.4 of the Bridge Manual permits the use of a TL3 barrier system. In accordance with Clause B6.2, where a semi-rigid barrier system is installed on a single-lane bridge, the transverse design loads may be taken as 67 % of those specified in Table B4 of the Bridge Manual. Furthermore, Clause A1.4 allows the working width on single-lane bridges to be reduced to 270 mm for semi-rigid barriers. Accordingly, the use of a proprietary top mounted TL4 Thriebeam guardrail system, such as RamShield High Containment HC barrier installed along the bridge top face at sides is considered acceptable. Thriebeam barrier is considered acceptable provision for the occasional presence of people as per Bridge Manual cl B2.9.

properties.Title: Tracks and Outdoor Visitor Structures - Boardwalk

content:
_Captured Wednesday, 3 April 2019 8:33 a.m. · General › General / Misc_

- Reference Documents
- SNZ HB 8630-2004 Tracks and Outdoor Visitor Structures Handbook
- Safety barrier to a deck located adjacent to a retaining wall at Oraka Beach Road, Mahia 2008-81
- Bridge-manual-pdf-complete-v3.4
- Comparison with Bridge Manual;
- Ashhurst Bridge - BM comparison - GM[93]
- Long span trail bridge
- SNZ HB 8630:2004 Tracks and Outdoor Visitor Structures
- A building consent is not required for work on decks, platforms, bridges, boardwalks and like structures where it is not possible to fall more than 1.5 metres.
- Note that a safety barrier is still required under Building Code clause F4 – Safety from falling where there is a fall of 1 metre or more.

- Comment closed following ‘NZ Transport Agency’ approval for the usage of the ‘post and wire’ barrier on Awakino project retaining walls as advised via NTC 277. and Considering clause 5.19.1(b) of the ‘NZTA Highway structures design guide’ specifies the requirement of F4/AS1 for retaining wall where people are occasionally present, and children are not likely

_Captured Saturday, 29 April 2023 12:10 AM · General › Bridge Design_

- Reference documents;
- Section_18_Structures , Cl 18.3, Page 14
- The design of minor bridges and boardwalks must comply with the Building Code.
- Compliance with Clauses B1 – Structure and B2 – Durability
- Compliance with Clause F4 – Safety From Falling
- A design life of 50 years
- An importance level of 2
- NZS 4121:2001 – ‘Design for Access and Mobility – Buildings and Associated Facilities’
- A uniformly distributed live load of at least 4.0 kPa must be adopted, together with concentrated actions
